alkylation reagent: Cysteine residues were alkylated by iodoacetamide to allow the recovery of Cys-containing peptides.
cleavage agent: the gel segment was washed, dried in a speed vacuum, and rehydrated with a buffer containing trypsin and incubated overnight.
fractionation method: the stained portion of the gel was cut into small pieces and reduced with dithiothreitol.
instrument: detected by an inline mass spectrometer (Thermo Elite).
ionization type: The eluted peptides were ionized by electrospray ionization.
labeling: In the label-free quantification by spectral-counting (SC) proteomics experiments.
reduction reagent: the gel segment was washed, dried in a speed vacuum, and rehydrated with a buffer containing trypsin and incubated overnight.
